OverviewSoftwareAboutGitHub

Bayesian Network

/ˈbeɪzɪən ˈnɛtˌwɜːk/
A probabilistic graphical model — a Directed Acyclic Graph of nodes representing random variables, with edges encoding conditional probability relationships.

The web reference with information and tutorials for learning about Bayesian Networks.

Bayesian Networks
Probabilistic Graphical Models
DAG
Conditional Probability
Inference

Overview

Follow the links below to access resources with abundant information on Bayesian Networks.

Brief Intro

A brief introduction to Graphical Models and Bayesian Networks by Kevin Murphy (University of British Columbia).

Bayesian Nets Tutorial

Constructing Bayesian networks from prior knowledge — Bayesian statistical methods for using data to improve models.

Wikipedia Reference

Comprehensive reference on Bayesian networks — structure, inference algorithms, applications, and history.

Software Implementations

There are numerous software implementations for Bayesian networks. The most popular are listed below.

Java SDK Required — Some packages require Java. Download the bundled version if you don't have Java installed.
Weka
Requires Java

The Weka machine learning package from Waikato University. Includes Bayesian network classifiers alongside classification, regression, clustering, and visualization tools.

More Info
Tetrad Project

Creates, simulates, estimates, tests, and searches for causal and statistical models. Sophisticated methods in a friendly interface — no programming knowledge required.

More Info

About

This site was built to help those who want to learn and develop a deep understanding of Bayesian Networks.

Companion to MachineLearning.js — interactive machine learning tools running entirely in your browser.
